D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

HDR Bracket Merge — Three Exposures → One Tone-Mapped Result

capture −2 EV / 0 EV / +2 EV; the merge weights each pixel by its “well-exposedness”, the tone-map compresses the merged radiance back into 8-bit

A bracketed HDR sequence captures the same scene at multiple exposures. The merge step combines them in linear radiance, weighting each pixel by how well-exposed it is in that frame (Debevec-Malik 1997 / Mertens 2007 style). The merged result has more dynamic range than 8-bit allows — a tone-mapping operator compresses it back, preserving local contrast.

The synthetic scene has a dark interior and a blown-out window. Each bracket shows one exposure; the merged + tone-mapped result on the right preserves both. Drag the tone-map strength to compare a flat compression vs an aggressive one.
